data.title: Nvidia-Backed Reflection AI Targets $25B Valuation—What It Means for AI Investors
data.description:
Reflection AI, a fast-growing startup backed by Nvidia (NVDA), is reportedly targeting a massive $25 billion valuation. According to The Wall Street Journal, the company is in talks to raise around $2.5 billion in new funding, which could significantly boost its valuation beyond its previous level. For investors, this isn't just another funding round. It highlights the strong demand for AI, especially in developer-focused tools, where big money continues to flow.

For context, Reflection AI was founded in 2024 by Google (GOOGL) DeepMind researchers. The company focuses on creating U.S.-based AI tools, including coding assistants, to boost transparency and compete with global AI players.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: JEF Earnings: Jefferies Financial Group Posts Record Revenue amid Takeover Speculation
data.description:
Jefferies Financial Group (JEF) has reported solid financial results on a day when media reports surfaced that the Wall Street investment bank is a takeover target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jefferies posted record revenues, although it missed analysts' profit expectations. The boutique investment bank reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.70, which fell short of the consensus forecast of $0.95. Revenue in the period totaled $2.02 billion, which matched analyst estimates.

Sales were up 27% year-over-year and were a record for the company. The latest results included a $36 million after-tax write-down of goodwill related to the announced sale of Tessellis and $17 million in losses from private credit firms Market Financial Solutions and First Brands.

The firm said that its investment banking revenues reached a record $1.02 billion during the quarter, up 45% from a year earlier and driven by improved underwriting performance. Capital markets revenues totaled $779 million, up 12% from a year ago, with equities posting record revenue of $558 million. Fixed income revenues declined to $220 million from $289 million a year earlier.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: GameStop's Q4 Earnings Prove the Rise of PC Gaming Is Bad for GME Stock
data.description:
GameStop (GME) stock could be in trouble soon, as a shift in gaming trends could leave the video game retailer with a shrinking customer base. The company reported its Q4 results after markets closed on Tuesday, and revenue for the holiday quarter fell 10.57% year-over-year to $1.1 billion. A major reason for this revenue drop was the game industry's shift away from physical games.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

PC gaming has largely been digital for over a decade now, as platforms such as Valve's Steam and the Epic Games Store offer direct digital sales to consumers. On top of that, PC gaming is growing. Some experts predict that PC gaming revenue will surpass console gaming revenue as early as 2028. This shift would further harm physical video game retailers, such as GameStop.

Finally, console gaming has also started to turn away from physical games. Instead, video game publishers have pushed for digital sales, which return bigger profits as no physical media has to be created. Adding to that trend are major companies offering subscription services, including Microsoft's (MSFT) Xbox Game Pass, Sony's (SONY) PlayStation Plus, and Nintendo's (NTDOF) Switch Online services. All of these offer games to subscribers without the need for a physical sale.

What Does This Mean for GameStop Stock?

If the shift to digital gaming continues to gain momentum, it would mean ongoing drops in revenue from physical game sales. This could spell trouble for GameStop, as video game sales make up a large portion of its revenue.

This isn't to say that GameStop is unaware of the issue. The retailer has been expanding into other avenues of pop culture to help prop up its sales. That includes buying and selling individual trading cards, such as Pokémon, Dragon Ball Super, Disney (DIS) Lorcana, Hasbro's (HAS) Magic: The Gathering, and sports cards. However, it only deals in graded cards, which limits its audience to collectors and not those who play trading card games.

Is GameStop Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of GameStop is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates GME stock as Neutral with a $23.50 price target. It cites "improving profitability and cash flow, supported by disciplined cost control, but tempered by ongoing revenue declines and a more risk-prone balance-sheet profile" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: 3 ETFs Analysts Back as Stagflation Jitters Mount amid the U.S.-Iran War
data.description:
Escalating conflict in the Middle East is driving up oil prices even as the U.S. labor market remains weak. This has prompted analysts to flag a renewed risk of a 1970s‑style bout of stagflation, a situation where economic growth weakens while inflation remains elevated.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This article highlights three exchange-traded funds (ETFs) investors can bank on to pull through such a scenario. The funds are the iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF (IYH), the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU), and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP).

The ETFs are considered solid defensive assets due to their inelastic demand, even during periods of economic slowdown, as they are critical to everyday survival. The funds were selected for their Buy rating and significant upside potential.

The iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF (IYH)

IYH provides investors with exposure to the broad U.S. healthcare industry by spreading its portfolio across various types of companies, from pharmaceutical and medical device firms to biotech companies and healthcare service providers.

Although the ETF has fallen about 7% since the start of the year, it holds about 24% upside and has a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts. This comes with an average price target of $76.21 and breaks down to 85 Buys, 17 Holds, and one Sell assigned over the past three months

The Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU)

As the name suggests, the XLU focuses on utility companies that provide electricity, water, natural gas, and wastewater services that are critical for economic productivity.

XLU has gained about 6% YTD and currently offers about 10% upside based on an average price target of $49.76. Analysts currently consider the ETF a Moderate Buy and have assigned 22 Buys and 10 Holds over the past three months.

Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP)

XLP targets another critical sector: consumer staples, which is where companies that produce everyday essential products and services such as food, beverages, household products, and personal items are active.

Analysts also consider XLP a Moderate Buy based on 24 Buys, 10 Holds, and three Sells issued over the past three months. However, the fund has risen about 5% since the start of the year and offers roughly 15% upside.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: AMD Stock Jumps on Reports of Incoming CPU Price Increases
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock soared on Wednesday alongside reports that the chipmaker will increase the price of its CPUs. The Nikkei Asia report claimed that both AMD and rival CPU maker Intel (INTC) will introduce CPU price increases in March and April. The report claims that these price increases will affect all series of CPUs offered by the semiconductor companies.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This report claims that the price increase for AMD and Intel CPUs is due to a classic case of supply and demand. The increasing demand for CPU components to power AI servers has stressed supply chains. As a result, the wait time for CPUs has increased dramatically, from one to two weeks to eight to 12 weeks. With this increased demand comes higher prices for those CPUs.

The report claims that the AMD and Intel CPU price increases will be an average of 10%-15%, and that some products will see even steeper prices increases. There's also talk that they won't be the last CPU price increases this year. Instead, insiders are expecting the price of CPUs to continue to rise in the following months as demand for AI data centers grows.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock was up 6.14% on Wednesday, extending a 1.78% year-to-date rally. The stock has also increased 86.38% over the past 12 months, largely due to the AI boom.

AMD trading activity today saw some 20 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 32.91 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Arm's AI Chip Fits "Core Strength in Low Power" — Stock Soars 16% on Massive Target Boosts
data.description:
Shares of British chip designer Arm Holdings (ARM) jumped over 16% on Wednesday as more analysts hailed its pivot towards AI chipmaking with the introduction of the AGI CPU. Wall Street greeted the move with massive price target boosts, with Barclays (BCS) noting that the technology taps into Arm's "core strength in low power."

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade NVDA with leverage
  • New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

The launch of the chip, which is expected to enter full production in the second half of this year, marks a shift in business model for Arm. The Cambridge-based company's core business has long been licensing out its chip designs to partners such as Nvidia (NVDA).

Now, Arms company is working with AI heavyweights Meta (META) and OpenAI to achieve the milestone.

Barclays Hails 'Strength in Energy Efficiency'

Reacting to the update, Barclays' five-star analyst Tom O'Malley lifted his ARM price target by about 21%, from $165 to $200, implying about 27% upside. The analyst also reaffirmed his Buy rating on Arm's shares, noting that the chip "plays into Arm's strength in energy efficiency."

O'Malley's note comes as the huge energy demand of AI chips and data centers has emerged as a key bottleneck to hyperscalers, forcing chipmakers and contract manufacturers alike into a race to develop energy-efficient chips. The analyst expects Arm to roll out more customer announcements and product launches related to the move and sees the pivot fetching upsides for the company into the end of the decade.

Evercore Sees Road to $15B Revenue

Chipping in, Evercore ISI analyst Mark Lipacis called Arm "a key beneficiary of agentic [or autonomous] AI," and sees the new chip helping to propel the chip designer to a revenue of $15 billion by fiscal 2031. The five-star analyst maintained his Buy rating on ARM, elevating his price target by a much larger 34%.

Lipacis lifted his price target from $170 to $227, implying about 45% upside.

Is ARM Holdings a Strong Buy?

Arm's shares currently enjoy a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 20 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell issued by 25 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average ARM price target of $170.86 only indicates about 9% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: This Little-Known Company Is the Best Oil Stock to Buy Now, Says UBS Group (UBS)
data.description:
Swiss bank UBS Group (UBS) has named a little-known company as its top pick in the oil sector.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to UBS, Ovintiv (OVV) is the best oil stock for investors to buy now as the war in Iran continues and crude oil prices fluctuate wildly. You can be forgiven if you haven't heard of Ovintiv. The company largely flies under the radar and isn't a household name like oil companies Chevron (CVX) and Shell (SHEL).

Based in Denver, Colorado, Ovintiv was known as Encana and based in Canada until 2020, when it relocated to the U.S. and underwent a rebrand. Today, Ovintiv is engaged in hydrocarbon exploration, with operations mostly in the Permian Basin of Texas.

Why OVV Stock?

So why is UBS bullish on OVV stock? The Swiss bank says the company has successfully addressed two concerns that previously weighed on its share price and valuation. The company's acquisition of NVA's Montney assets, combined with its recently announced Anadarko Basin divestiture, has largely resolved issues related to crude oil and condensate inventory as well as its high debt levels.

UBS' bullish call comes with UVV stock up 47% this year, riding high on the back of surging crude oil prices. The war in Iran has sent crude oil prices as high as $120 a barrel in recent weeks. Brent crude oil, the international standard, is currently trading at $102 per barrel amid signs of a diplomatic resolution to the Middle East war.

Is OVV Stock a Buy?

Ovintiv stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average OVV price target of $59.12 implies 0.92% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Spotlight on TSLA, SERV, RR Stocks after Robot Showcase at the White House
data.description:
American robot makers, such as Tesla (TSLA), Serve Robotics (SERV), and Richtech Robotics (RR), might have just gotten another sign of President Donald Trump's focus on the sector. On Wednesday, First Lady Melania Trump showcased "Figure 03," which she called the first U.S.-made humanoid guest at the White House.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

The robot walked down a hallway in the White House as part of the Fostering the Future Together Summit hosted by the American first lady, who harped on the role of artificial intelligence in education.

U.S. Races to Counter Chinese Robotics Domination

The event comes after the U.S. Department of Commerce and the think tank, the Council on Foreign Relations, earlier this month hosted roundtable discussions on ways to boost the local robotics industry and secure America's leadership in the field. Attendees at the first one called for a national robotics strategy and standards and emphasized the need for safety, cybersecurity, and effective data management.

Both events came following reports in December that efforts are underway by the Trump administration to issue an executive order to boost support for and accelerate the country's domestic robotics industry, even as China continues to make inroads in the field and dominates the market.

China Doubles Down on Robotics Race

The latest event comes even as China continues to push for domination in humanoid robots, with the Asian economic giant recently warning that the industry now faces the risk of a humanoid robot bubble. Chinese startups such as Unitree Robotics, Galbot, Noetix, and MagicLab have emerged as some of the leading robotics development companies in the country.

Last month, humanoid robots from China were a standout attraction at the CCTV Spring Festival Gala, the country's most-watched television program. 

What Is the Best Robotics Stock to Buy?

Of the three robotics stocks mentioned in this article, Richtech Robotics currently offers the biggest upside of about 175% based on an average price target of $60, according to TipRanks' Stock Comparison tool. This is even though RR stock is rated a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market Review Today: SPY, QQQ Rebound as U.S.-Iran Exchange Ceasefire Terms; Trump Reschedules Xi Summit
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) in the green on Wednesday as optimism over a U.S.-Iran ceasefire pushed investor sentiment higher.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • The U.S. and Iran have each outlined their conditions to end the war, although major disagreements remain on both sides.
  • The U.S. called on Iran to cease uranium enrichment, end its nuclear weapons program, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz as a free maritime corridor. Iran's proposal includes war reparations, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and an end to "aggression and assassinations" by the U.S. and Israel.
  • President Trump rescheduled his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping to May 14-15. The meeting, originally scheduled for March 31 to April 2, was pushed back due to the war in Iran.
  • Trump signed an executive order to create the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST). The council will advise the administration on developments in AI, quantum computing, and biotechnology and will include the CEOs of Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Google (GOOGL), among others.
  • A jury ruled that Meta and Google are responsible for creating platforms that pose risks to teenagers.
  • AMD (AMD) surged 7% following reports that the company would hike CPU prices by an average of 10%-15%.
  • UBS lowered its Microsoft (MSFT) price target to $510 from $600, citing competition in the AI space and poor sentiment surrounding the company's M365 and Copilot services.
  • BlackRock (BLK) CEO Larry Fink warned of a "stark and steep recession" if oil pushes above $150.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

The materials sector was the best-performing group on Wednesday as hopes for a U.S.-Iran ceasefire contributed to lower oil prices. Cheaper oil reduces production costs for metals, chemicals, and other materials companies and helps support stronger profit margins for the sector.

Several stocks led the materials sector's gains, including:

  • International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF)
  • Amcor (AMCR)
  • PPG Industries (PPG)
  • Dow Inc. (DOW)
  • Mosaic Company (MOS)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the energy sector faced the largest drawdown. Energy stocks often move inversely with materials stocks when oil rises or falls significantly. Higher oil prices boost energy profits but raise costs for materials companies, while lower oil prices reduce energy revenues and improve margins for materials firms.

Notable energy stocks trading lower include:

  • Valero Energy (VLO)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Baker Hughes (BKR)
  • Chevron (CVX)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Steady after Middle East Peace Hopes Lift the Market
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were flat on Wednesday evening after a strong regular session, aided by growing optimism over potential progress toward peace in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.05%, 0.06%, and 0.04%, respectively, at 6:34 p.m. EDT on March 25.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Major stock indices gained in the regular trading session amid cautious optimism about a potential ceasefire in the Middle East and a drop in energy prices. The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 rose 0.8%, 0.7%, and 0.5%, respectively.

Looking ahead, traders await the latest initial jobless claims for the week ending March 21. On the earnings front, Blink Charging (BLNK) and Pony AI (PONY) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
